I think that there should be more check in emotions like fear or jealousy
The question: Do you feel sad or down? Needs a third Answer,like: I don't know(How I feel).

It's a really good app. It has an easy database and stores questions and has them relatively easy to answer. I redownloaded the app as I joticied my mental health was going. It is a great tool and I highly suggest it! Especially if you almost always have your device with you. I like to take like five minutes each question period to do that and some relaxing thing to just.. ground myself.
Would be better if some of the all the time options were "sick" and "injured". I might be doing okay when I am sick mentally but I don't feel "good" and I would like to mark why.

After all the questions you answer that they would probably have to pay a lot of money for research for that. You answer a lot of questions only to you know, changing and they have sub plans like 90 a year. IMHO I don't know if I would do it but it does seem like a good app and scientific, maybe I'm just looking into too much. I think I'll read the bible more.

my first review was 5 stars i had to make it 3 because it's disappointing that i can't even see my previous. i'm keeping it like a diary and sometimes i write paragraphs and not being able to re-read what i wrote is something i don't like. if you fix this I'll give 5 stars again

[UPDATE 2.0: it works again!!] it works really well for me and helps a lot in keeping track of how i feel throughout the day. i feel like there can be more options at the end (eg bored, lazy, calm, etc), but there is still a good amount as of now. great app!! (:

I am only a week and a half into this and it is great so far. It asks relevant questions about my mood etc and how I react to situations and gives me insights to help me tackle different areas. I am finding it good to be able to feed my moods back too as it helps me to realise where I need to work on because when you are feeling anxious/depressed everything seems to hit you harder than it should. You can feel like the smallest of things are a massive hurdle and this app is really helping me.

I've been using it for over a year now, and as much as I love it it's really annoying when it asks irrelevant questions. If we could submit a profile so the questions are more relevant, I feel like the app would be more accurate.
Best mood tracking app. It gives you a report at the end of your emotional state every two weeks. I really like the other features too like the courses and the meditation.

Overall I like this app. It has helped me a bit to understand what I'm feeling. However the notification to write down how I feel and answer the questions rarely comes. I find very frustrating when I do recieve the notification, answer the question and write how I feel and why and then it kind of glitches and it doesn't saves. Also the questions are very repetitive. After I month I don't feel inclined to answer the same question every day.
Boring, repeating questions about myself that I don't like telling anyone. Constantly wa ting me to pay for a substitution.

Cannot recommend this app enough, it has changed the way I reflect on my experiences and helps me find correlations in my behaviour and mood, provides a historical look at how you've been and where you've come from. I'm giving it 4 stars for two reasons, a) the question pool it asks you from has gotten repetitive after 3 months and it feels like it will ask you those same questions almost every day, and b) you can't add extra positive and negative emotions, you're stuck with very limited choices
i like this app, but as a teenager i can buy more options so that's a minus

The daily prompts to record how you're feeling is very useful. I like that the questions are specific rather than me having to write a journal entry, which I can find overwhelming and doesn't track your mood. This app, however, does. I also like the useful insights which I find informative.

This app is incredibly useful because the way it stands out from other mood trackers is the questions. As a person who sometimes has trouble recognizing how they really feel, like becoming more and more depressed but putting the mood assay neutral the two week results from answering the questions clued me into that things were not okay. Minus one start because the reminders never stay on schedule regardless of settings and can go way off.

I am using the app since over a year now as a mood tracker and diary. You can export all data to an excel sheet. Much later I discovered all the other options like meditations which are really nice with long breaks and for different topics, sleeping, fear, sadness. There are plenty. Also a lot of background information about depression and emotions and how to deal with them. I really really like this app and fully recommend it.
Nice app but I do not like the purchases to unlock more features

I enjoy the concept of this app but i need a reminder if i must input info 3 X's daily. Personally, I feel like one in depth check in would be sufficient and an expanded emotional declaration section might be more insightful. TY 😸
I really like this app but I preferred the old logo/name. Can you make a setting optional?

MoodPath "noticed" when I was doing better and let me know! It felt like I was getting positive reinforcement from someone. As in "seems like you've been doing better recently" and I thought - yes I *have* been! Look at that! It gave me more confidence for the rest of the week.

The good thing here is that I feel like I can talk to them even though I don't speak, I just write my feelings as if someone already understands me when I'm not in a good mood. great help for me. But I can't subs for full ver. If I dont have money, sad :(

A peaceful and secured little oasis for me to reflect on myself safely. I really like the biometric unlock feature, well, of course no one would have my phone and check it, but just the feature itself makes me feel more at ease :) thank you❤️
Please can you add more tags like for anxiety because "afraid" doesn't really explain how I feel.

This app is so good! I have been using it for only two days and i smile when ever they want to ask me a question! This app id making me feel like someone care about me and that someone really care about how i feel! Edit: i have been using it for over a month now and i'm starting to feel better It really helps a lot and i hope that everyone will feel better like me❤

Honestly, this app helped me. A lot. Even though I'm still on the free one, the app helps me to track my condition. With the questions and insight, I feel like I get to to know how I am a little better. The article are easy to understand. Last but not least, kudos to the developer to actually answering my email for help :) thank you. One problem that I got earlier is that sometimes I wrote my journals pretty long, but it didn't saved. But nowadays they're more likely to me saved. I'm loving it.

As soon as I started I've actually felt more open to share my feelings with people close to me but I'm also trying to find out if I have depression or anxiety I'm only 13 years nearly 14 in January 18 but I've always felt different like weird compare to other people I just feel like a freak so I came to the app to see if I do have problems and I think I may but at same time idk if I do so hopefully the app gives me the right answer

I really enjoy having the ability to see any improvement and it helps me to not neglect certain aspects of my health. I also like being able to send a report to my doctor as it helps figure out if I need a change of treatment or meds.
I like this app very comfortable and convenient to use. I find it helps to identify how you feel while tracking your mood at the same time.

I like this app, it's really good for keeping track of your mental health. However, I find a little bit rude the "At the moment you're answering the minimum number of questions. Try answering more questions.", it's like they're mad at you if you don't answer the questions daily and it puts a lot of pressure on you. Overall it's great👍🏻

I don't really look at the results because past 15 results are Indications of a severe depressive episode, and I did have depression before, but not anymore. I feel like app deserves attention, if you have problems tracking your emotions, or if you want to keep in touch with your mental health

Also really like that you can choose certain emotions and write a little note/blurb for yourself too. No ads is a great bonus. Sometimes find it frustrating that must pick yes OR no as sometimes I'm neither or in the middle. All in all though, this is a great app.

I really like this App, it's Simple, no ads and just good looking. I dislike that you have to answer yes/no. Sometimes i just don't know the answer, because i have really confused feelings. So it would be nice if there were Button with 'A little bit' or 'i don't know' or something like this. Maybe it's the Point to decide if yes or no but sometimes you are just in between.
Yeah! Would have loved it more but like I'm poor.

They don't even try to find any other help for the ones "they can't help".
It actually helps me mentally it's like a diary but you don't have to keep writing

Then, when I'm feeling a little better I look back at history and see how down I was, which motivates me to improve. Its not perfect... but the overall impact is positive.

This app is really helpful for helping me track my mental health and notice patterns or common issues or area of struggle. Thank you for this app, it could not be more helpful x

The insights ranged from obvious to too broad to be helpful. The questions felt like the app was playing a guessing game about my mental health, but wasn't really THAT interested in delving too deep into my mental health. The mood tracking wasn't nearly as in depth as it needed to be for it to be useful. 2 weeks later and I'm right where I was.
Great for focusing on my mental health and life skills throughout the day

Looks like a lovely and worthwhile app for Mental health. However, the subscription is too much. I am afraid not everyone can afford this. Maybe this should be free or discounted to an affordable price?4

I think this app is AMAZING. And the fact it is in connection with Betterhelp is awesome and why I feel it is credible to use as helping those in need. The feature where it gives u an evaluation of how ur mental health has changed i really like because I have always had a problem with keeping track of times my overall mental health is doing good. I've lived many years believing I'm not happy and so the times that I am in good moods I always believe it's temporary and that's what keeps me stuck.
The beat mental health app I've ever used!

Honestly this app is amazing ! I have been using it for lil over a year now and honestly it's been a major help in dealing with bpd and bad mental health it isn't everything but in myself it's great to get to see where I range to and from the insights are really helpful i have recommended this app to numerous friends over the time I've used it many say the same and i still preach about it whether your happy or depressed it doesn't matter this honestly has worked wonders, stick to it 🥰🥰
Some of the questions are irrelevant to me about my mental health. And some I just don't understand fully? Maybe it's just me? But other than that the app is good.
Been using this app for years to keep track of my mental health. Definitely helps me be aware of how I'm feeling, so I can be mindful in my actions through the day

Using MindDoc for a year+, paid version, crazy helpful for tracking mental health in a stats way. Downloadable pdfs bi-weekly so I can share w my Healthcare team. Personal assurance that I'm doing okay, and tips for taking care of yourself are invaluable. 100% worth the yearly sub.
Very good service-rendering app in mental health care, so far. I would love that its help being offered continue.

Been using this for 2 years now. The app is great. Developments are well thought of and gives much more support rather than the simplistic style it had before (journal and mood tracker based analysis of mental health). Now, the insights do help and you get more thoughts on what things you should be mindful of.
This app is a key part of how I look after my mental health. I appreciate the space to document how I'm feeling and what I'm thinking.
Provides great mental health insight. Easy to follow sections on Anxiety, Depression, Emotions... Daily questions & prompts. Not unreasonably priced.
This app is great, if you wanna keep a small journal and see how mood/mental health is evolving, this is perfect.
Would definitely reccomend. This app helps you analyze and try to strengthen your mental health 💚
Great app but it would help if it could automatically plot mods changes over time to help mental health professionals

One of the better mental health apps I've tried recently. The insights could be better but I also have been googling things for a while now so if you're new to mental health, maybw they're more exciting to read. But the calendar and mood tracking are great and have been really helpful. Edit: The colors on the health tracking questions make it easy to lie if you don't want to be bothered by the app in low moments but just want the notif, because the blue option never asks for more details.

MindDoc is an immaculate mental health and emotional support and mood tracking app thta I HIGHLY RECOMMEND to anyone who wishes to record their day to day experience and have a detailed analysis of a 15 day cycle; Morning, afternoon and evening, as well as GREAT insight material for mindfulness, and understanding ones mental health, keep up the amazing work, Nameste 🙏
I think it's the best mental health app I've ever used. Would reccomend.
A very interesting way of doing mental health. Thorough and professional.
It helps me keep a check on my mental health
Working again, for sure loving it, great app to track my mental health condition and to understand my feelings, emotions and myself better.
Helps me track my mental health, easy to use and very comfortable! Gives multiple options to help with said mental health.

Now all of a sudden when I get a notification that I have a new insight it's locked and I guess I'd have to pay to read it. Not very helpful in a crisis situation. Are there any truly free mental health apps that aren't trying to sell me something?

I fell on this app by chance and I recommend it. The app has been helping me meanwhile I find a good psychologist. I am not paying for premium content by the way. Only the free content has been fairly helping me. So I strongly recommend it!! Wish everybody good luck in his/her mental health struggles!

I wish there was a bit more reflection, and that the questions adapted more based on how you answer. Regardless, it's a nice way to track your mental health, and the question bank seems to have expanded recently.
It's great app! I'm glad that it exists in Internet) Now i know enough information about my mental health, and I feel better when I keep a diary about my mood. MindDoc, thank you that you are!
Honestly, I really appreciate keeping track of my mental health and this app has such a neat and user friendly design and UI that makes it easy and neat to log good days and bad days.

What I love most about this app is the specific questions it asks. Reflecting on those makes it a lot easier to target how my symptoms show up and sometimes even the reason behind I'm feeling negatively. I've been holding myself much more accountable for taking care of my mental health since getting this. Would recommend.
Good app to track and maintain your mental health
Love it that it alerts me when I'm beginning to spiral. Useful to have a broad overview of my mental health that I can take to my doctor.

Very good application...helpful with just about the right questions... One suggestion- with time the questions should evolve...more research should be done in depth for better guidance of mental health patients
It has really helped me understand my thoughts and mental health.
Pretty good mental health app. I like the mood tracking feature, and it doesn't take much time at all, but the questions can be somewhat anodyne and repetitive. Still, it helps!
Revolutionary for mental health awareness, I would love to be a part of a mental health awareness movement

MindDoc is the best mental health tracker I've used. I used to use Daylio for a while, but I prefer MindDoc primarily for the 3x a day check-ins. My emotions and mental state fluctuate wildly throughout the day and I like to be able to track that, instead of trying to summarize my overall mood for the day. My favorite feature is just the ability to visualize my mood history, and I appreciate the questions asked at every check-in. It helps me gauge my mental health in a different way.

This is a good app, helps me look back how I spent my day and all, but why isn't there an option of angry or mad in the moods section? There should be one because I have anger issues and sometimes I get really angry when I deal with a conflict and I think that effects my mental health

This app has been really helpful to my mental health. I've been waiting on therapy and my friend recommended it to me and I look forward to the questions and insights. It helps me stay grounded and in the present and keeps me from staying on issues for as long as I used to.
I have used many mental health tracker apps and I usually get bored within after a while but this one has kept me going and it's very helpful to see the results and the month by month display
It's a great way to track your mental health, and gives you tons of info for free
It's a great way to visibly see your overall mood dips and rises. The daily questions are great for keeping tabs on one's mental health
This is a very good app. I can get my weekly and fortnightly mental health analysis. The insights that are available are extremely important and proved very helpful for me.
I seriously recommend people to use it. Not only because you feel depresed but for also to have a check that is ur mental health alright?

This app has allowed me to track how I am doing and is like a quick journal to look back on. The insights and things like that are helpful and encouraging. This app has continued to act as a great tool to just keep my mental health in check. It also has a great feature to share your results with your therapist. 10/10 recommend.
I wish it was more in tune with other mental health issues rather than just depression. As someone with multiple mental illnesses it would be nice to have it help track those as well.
Realy helpful to track your daily emotions and feelings and is great because they give you a 14 day report every 14 days wich can help know if your mental health is improving or not
Excellent app! This app has helped me figure out my own mental health and has pushed me to seek help when necessary! Highly recommend!
MindDoc is a good App Companion. It keeps you abreast about your Mental Health and gives you sound advice. It also warns you to seek help when they can't.

Been using it for about a year now, and it's like having a friend check up on you every few hours. It helps you reflect deeply on how you feel and asks you questions to allow yourself to find areas of you to improve on. Helplines are available and so are overall results to sum up your mental health every 2 weeks. I love it :)

Features that made it what it is are now largely paywalled. Inc personalized "emotion" tags & data visualization (orig. winding path with mood markers, chart tracking mood patterns thru day/month). Although the motivations are clear, it still stands that a tool dedicated to mental health should not skimp on its accessibility. Otherwise, where do the goalposts lie? In maximizing $ or in providing adequate help to others? Depressingly hilarious iteration of the need for this tool to begin with.
Stupid pop psych BS and other nonsense. You gotta be a fool to trust an app to evaluate your mental health.
It's a good app to track your emotions and mental health
I think this is my favourite app of all time. This is the first time I feel like a mental health app has actually helped me. Keep it up

One of the few apps that offers substantive mental health and wellness content without a subscription. Regular checkins, feedback, and insights. I have a good sense of what they offer and plan to invest in the subscription ASAP.
it's helping me with my mental health

This is the best mental health app I've used so far, and I've probably tried upwards of ten. I usually stop using them because the energy of writing an entry eventually fades and then I just feel guilty so having a mental health app that you can just answer questions instead is genius. I wish there was an app like this for things other than depression or it was actually more anxiety focused.
This app has really helped me keep track of my emotions and mental health progress and I really recommend it.
I am amazed by how wonderful this app is. So many insights, it has definitely helped my mental health immensely.
If you have real mental health issues then give this app a go.
Amazingly designed app with ahuge benefit for your mental health. I couldn't recommend it more!

I like the app but only a psychologist should assess mental health not an app. That is the reason for 1 star. So feel free to ask questions about sleep, or moods but never try to assess a persons mental health with an app, it is a dangerous thing to do without a therapists over watch. Leave out the assessment and focus on the tools for certain disorders that have scientific backing through research. Who are the experts? Put links to actual research and put your experts in the description.
Really basic information. I guess if you were just starting to learn about mental health this could be helpful, but as a mental health professional this was not useful or helpful at all.
Very useful for looking at long term mental health and its patterns.
Great app for mental health.
i love the structure of this app, it's so easy to keep track of your mood and it also gives you helpful insights and resources for mental health. really makes it feel like a friend.
It helps you find ur mental health and gives you some advice
its a great way to track my mental health amd help me improve.

I am loving the mental health review and the opportunity to export that to share with my psychologist to help me further. The questions asked actually help me to think about how I'm feeling better, yet the process is so simple I can do it quickly. The fortnightly reports also help provide insights to how you are doing in clear language.
It's been helpful in my mental health struggles. I can't go to therapy, so this is still quite helpful.

After using the app for 2 weeks it gave me my first "result" and determined that I was experiencing a severe depressive episode. After this, it went from asking me 10+ questions about my mental health every day to only 2/3, which is NOT helpful to me at all. I don't understand why it would ask LESS about your mental health even after it's decided you are severely depressed - when you need help the most. It also repeats questions constantly (it'll ask me if I'm lgbtq+ every couple of days...).
Superb. What else can you download that is capable of changing the way you frame your mental health?

This app is fantastic for tracking your moods and letting you know when to seek help. It provides contact info for helplines you may need, and ways to help with the way you feel. My mental health has improved since using this (and I only have the free version)

It is a very thoughtful app especially for mental health awareness. It keeps track of your mood and analyzed what are you lacking of and it gives you the article that can help you to find out more about yourself. ❤
Really good way to keep track of your mental health and with good advice on how to help
This app has been very helpful for me and helps me to check in with my mental health daily. The insights that it gives are also very helpful!
Love it helps with my mental health
This helpes me si much specially in my daily mental health.

A nice app for tracking your mental health over time. Answering questions is easy and you can set when notifications ping. The articles and information provided are almost always relevant to any problems you've been having. The details of your mood and activities are a bit limited, but you can add your own and write a note, which is nice. It's a helpful tool for anybody, regardless of your mental health.

I wish that this app gave you access to a therapist that is onsite. I am younger and have a bit of trouble getting my parents to believe me about my mental health. I wish that I could have someone to talk to that is not an automated machine.

This app has everything set in place both for people willing to stay on track with mental health or for those with severe mental issues. You don't feel super guilty and bad for missing days however it keeps you consistent throughout the week for a report. I would've given it 5 stars had it been a little more advanced with questions related to health problems. There can be a lot going on apart from mental health, so I find asking about it everyday is important. Can you use it on both devices tho?
This app is brilliant.. if you find it hard to talk to mental health or poeple in general and have suicidlal thoughts then install this app!!!! 🙄

This app, has helped me not only understand my mental health, but helped me understand that I am not alone...by asking questions such as: Do you feel worth about yourself a lot right now? This let's me know that other people aren't the only one's yes.
Overall a pretty good app, wish some things were more customizable without having to pay but other than that it's nice to keep track of your mental health
It's a useful tool to track mood cycles of you have a mental health disorder like bipolar.
Very helpful to keep track of moods and learn about mental health.
This helped me managed my mental health.

I think it's really helpful and shows you alot about your mental health and how to improve on it. I would recommend this app to anybody who has anxity or depression or any other mental health issues! :)

It's a helpful app. It's a journal and a therapist. It asks you questions about your mood, feelings and thoughts thrice a day. It notes down these insights on a daily basis. You can express yourself freely without any fear of judgement. It has a section which has information about mental health topics, sleep and positivity. It also has an auto-generated chat section. You can express yourself there and it will help you. It can also connect you to professional therapists via call It's a great app

I really enjoy using this app. Very useful and helpful for tracking my day to day mental health condition. Moreover, it has insights to give me information, reminder, and guidance to do mindful breathing exercise. I love it so far!
A very helpful tool to monitor your mental health.

Moodpath is a great app for keeping track of your mental health. It asks you questions every day, and gives you feedback on them after 14 days. It tells you if you have risk of depression or not, and if you need professional help. 10/10.
Great App! I thought I was kinda depressive, but I am not. The review told me it's normal and gave me insightful insights so as to improve my mental health and quality of life.

So far im pretty impressed. I have mental health issues and need to build a better mindset without having a person to hold me accountable 24/7. This app is good for so many things and I'm really leaning towards getting the premium to help me while I am on leave trying to heal my broken brain. I also recently found that you are able to also share your findings from the app with your doctor. This could be a really innovative solution to helping with mental health wellbeing.

Kinda sad that you only put "have you been trying to lose weight" when theres a lot of us who are skinny and are pressured to gain weight to the point we develop eating disorders like I did almost. Nobody cares about us who overeat and overexert ourselves with exercises just to be bigger and sexier, so people stop telling us how ugly we're for being skinny. As a mental health app, you should know better than this.
Its nice to get things down. And it helps me be more aware of my mental health

It does what it says. Awesome tool to keep track of your emotions and take care of your mental health. Feels nice when it asks periodically how do I feel. Must have if you are searching something great for well being of your emotional health.
Great for keeping track of your mental health. Easy to read and understand

This app is overall okay but if you forget to answer too many questions, it starts over and you have to do your assessment again. This isn't for people with busy schedules or that can't check their device that often. I for one, just forget to do certain things a lot, but you shouldn't have to struggle to get results, especially when it comes to mental health. The sooner that you can find out what is wrong, the sooner that you can get help or at least find ways to cope.
This app asks helpful questions each day to help me think about different aspects of my mental health. It's great for looking at trends to see if I can find patterns for my mood.

Lower maintenance mood tracker that checks on multiple types of mental health systems. Only change I'd prefer is to have the premium be a larger one time purchase. I want to support the app, and like those features, but can't afford subscription apps.
Very good at tracking mental health... Allows you to be more mindful
This app is so useful and has helped me with my mental health and overall happiness. I would very much recommend this app.

Professional, insightful, it's been incredibly helpful, more do than any other mental health app I've tried. There was no way I think I would've realized i had been dealing with a moderate or minor deppressive episode without it. It's been really helpful to see symptoms I wouldn't have noticed from the questions it asks, and it's been the biggest help in my road to recovery for my mental health.
helped alot with my mental health especially during the pandemic.
Good app to keep track of your own mental health. Helps to see if you are having more down days than up and potentially get help before these things become a huge problem

Nice beginner app for mental health awareness. It makes you aware about your feelings and compells you to know the state of your mental health. And it's functions and interface ain't overwhelming. It's very simple to answer the questions asked and based on that get your basic mental health status.
Very helpful insight to my mood, as well as something I have to regularly keep up with, which helps my mental health.

This app is optimal for tracking and understanding your feelings over time. I really love it (Don't worry this isn't one of those fake reviews😂) I honestly recommend it to people struggling with depression or somethin similar. It can help you see trends in your mental health and other issues. It's worth the download.❤
Amazing addition to my mental health routine.

I downloaded the app to help me keep track of my mental health because I have some diagnosed conditions I need to manage. While it sometimes points out obvious-to-me things like "oh hey you might have depression", the daily journal entries have really helped me figure out patterns in my moods. This has helped me develop much more effective coping strategies. The tracking alone makes it a good app; I haven't explored any of the premium features yet.
This is a very helpful app and makes me think of how I am behaving and my mental health
Great app to track your mental health state

Amazing App, you will get the best results if you use it for some time. But it has really helped me reflect on my thoughts and feelings. HUGE PLUS for the "coronavirus-extra"! They ask you questions specifically about the pandemic/self isolation, because that can really influence people with mental health issues. Great work!!

As a free service, this app is helpful in monitoring general moods and feelings. Allowing for personal reflection to the 'why' one may be feeling a certain way. That said, as a source of psychological assistance I'm disappointed that there is a pay wall with no opportunity for a free trial of all the services available to the app. For those who can't afford mental health services, another mental health app with another pay wall with the promise of assistance, to me, is disappointing.
The insights are quite helpful, and give concrete suggestions on how to handle your problems. I feel like it's a good aid to good mental health
I don't have depression or anxiety, but this app does a good job at helping me keep an eye on my mental health.
Really good for keeping tabs on changes. Helps me keep my mental health in check!
Excellent way to keep track of mental health on the day to day

I appreciate the periodic check in daily. It helps me assess how I'm actually feeling and find triggers that may be there. It also allows me to better track my data to get a fuller picture of my mental health.
A great help 4 mental health
It really help to track my emotions every day and make me more aware of my feelings and my mental health
Best mental health app
This app has been great for me to check in on myself and my mental health. I'd recommend this to anyone that's trying to track how their moods fluctuate. Helps keep me level with the checking in.
Found this app helpful fir mental health.
It helps me take time to actually realize how much mental health is important
Simple app to keep you thinking about your mental health

I have C-PTSD and am a peer support specialist at my husband's therpay practice. I have been looking for an app that would do exactly has this has done for me. The ability to check in multiple times a day is more accurate for a person wanting to use this to improve mental health. I am very impressed and will recommend to clients I work with. Thank you!
A good way to monitor your mental health through statistical calculation. Just wished it didn't have to cost ~$25 to get the whole package, though.
Has really helped me to manage and track my mental health. I show my therapist the weekly graph and that helps me to take the next step to recovery!

After reading the privacy policy I discovered that the app has facebook software development kit integrated in app, which means that all data from app is accessible for facebook. I don't recommend installing it if you don't want your mental health data used against you and sold to advertisers.
Very helpful in keeping me in check with my mental health

This app is very helpful for people who want to keep track of their moodespecially if they notice that their mood has changed in a way that interferes with their life. Moodpath can generate referral letters for you, and you can connect to a therapist through the BetterHelp app or through a hotline number in case of an emergency. The only thing I wish was that you could pause monitoring for one mental health concern so that you can monitor another, or monitor two at a time.
The functionality is good. Not sure if its beneficial for my mental health or not yet.

Although I sometimes struggle to keep up with entering my thoughts every day, I really like this app. Its helped me to stop and really think about how I feel every day, which has helped me to process by emotions better. I've noticed a definite difference in the way I look at my own mental health since I've been using this app. It's not an alternative to proper medical help by any means, but if you're not sure where to start with dealing with mental health I would definitely reccommend this app.

This app has helped me so much in my life. It enables me to keep track of my mental health without me having to put what I feel into words. The questions that you're asked not only help you track your state of mind and your place in life, but they also open your eyes to the way your mind and body are responding to your mental illness. It helps you realise when and why you need help. If you're feeling overwhelmed by your thoughts and emotions or feeling numb - get this app. Trust me. Just do it.
Really helpful on giving me ideas about what I should pay attention to in my mental health. It offers sociological help and things to help you calm down. I really like it

This app is great and it's completely free! You check in with your mood and answer a handful of questions a few times a day (all private of course), you can look at any of the material, there is a course you can follow, depending on what you feel you need (like if you are exhausted, or if you want to increase strength mentally and physically, etc). Highly recommended!

I had a very disappointing experience. As a mood tracker, it is too basic compared with a multitude of other apps. It's USP? It asks you questions 3 times a day for 2 weeks, at the end, you are told you need to consult a therapist. Which surprise, surprise, Minddoc can recommend one at $65 per week. It is utterly useless for me being unable to afford this and also in the UK. An app designed to exploit people's mental health fears for money
installed 3 days ago and i like it so far. though it's checkin on me by asking questions only
This app proveides lots of questions that made me reflect on my day to day mood and also plenty of tips to face some particular situations.

I was a little hesitant that I was going to lose interest in this but I haven't and I don't think I will. I'm reminded 3 times a day to answer a couple questions on my mental health and then I describe how I'm feeling in this moment and I'm really enjoying it!
I like it, 3 times a day it asks how you're doing, so throughout the day you're basically doing small Journaling entries.

I didn't expect this app to be as good as it is. It's helped me navigate through the aftermath of my panic disorder and gave me helpful tools for the future. The questions being asked 3 times a day can be overwhelming at times, but what I've found is that if you're busy or need to take a break from it, the app recognizes that and stops sending notifications after being ignored for a few days. I ended up buying the annual subscription and have recommended it to many of my friends and loved ones!

I've been trying to keep a journal for years and I'm just terrible at it. I've been using this app for about 5 weeks and this is the first time I've successfully kept up with something like this! It's been very helpful to track my mood and I like that I can go back and read my entries. I do wish the "emotions" board had some more options to choose from but it's not bad. The push notifications 3 times a day is very helpful, I usually get at least one entry in per day because of the reminders.

This app is exactly what I needed to help me with my anxiety and depression. The app asks questions 3 times a day and tracks your mood. It then gives you insights on how to make situations better. There are also classes to help you manage your life a lot better. I tried to speak to a counselor today but was told it would be 3 months before I could even get in. But this app is so amazing and insightful that I believe that this is all I need. This is a must have!

Take your mental health serious. Please. I've tried most apps, they all fall short (or achieve) somewhere but the questions used on a 3 times per day basis is good on this app. They need to broaden the questions though I think. Access to Betterhelp was convenient. Plus the app spotted something everything else missed.... Thanks.... I guess.
It's amazing. I'm using the free trial right now but I am using it every chance I get. And answer my questions 3 times a day. I love the insights and courses it has! 5 stars!
A very helpful app. Being able to set up a reminder for 3 times a day to record your current mindset helps me a lot. I am very happy with this app.

I've been using this app since 2018. You answer a few questions 3 times per day for 2 weeks, and at the end it gives you a printable report. It's been very helpful to track my emotional state and report to my mental health team.
It got me through some troubled times
Great app. I enjoy the amount of questions it has. It asks relevant questions to understand your mindset and helps you map your mood three times a day. Premium is a but expensive in my opinion

In the free version there are daily questions that you're supposed to answer 3 times a day to identify patterns in your mood. It shows you courses that you can buy but there are not many resources for free. It is somewhat helpful to be able to view your answers for each set of questions and notice patterns.

I love being able to check in 3 times a day. I very much like the set up of the app. I've been using it for well over 2months now. Only thing I wish is that I could view my notes all together in one space.

The worst part is it claims good things as bad, e.g. "I set realistic goals." and there's no way to enter them as good things, so it always errs on poor emotional health/ moderate, never "good". It's easy to use but quite annoying that you have to do it three times a day.
One of my 3 favorite apps I use. Simple. Clean to use and gives great line of site on mental health over time with 3 readings a day that take minimal time. I'd reccomend this app to anyone

The insights tell me what I already know but it's great as a symptom tracker because of the 3 reminders each day, mood tags, and extra notes option. At first the questions are really generic but the app will quickly adapt and start giving you more personalized/relevant ones. Now I get called out every time I open it lmao
It's like someone checking up on me 3 times a day, this app is the best.
Has helped me look and think deeper about my moods and behaviors day to day

I love the checking in a few times a day sometimes it's good to remeber you again great app just some questions I don't understand and sometimes it's not a yes or no answer. But I do love that it have a function if your at your lowest point to get you help and reminds you for a few mins a few times a day to check in on how your feeling. Been so helpful for myself Nd my mental health. Highly recommended x
Great keeping up with reminding me to check my mood 3 times a day and then reflects back on everything and helps
i really like the 14day review of emotional health it gives. its really helpful and 3 times a day isn't annoying to do

This is the first free mood tracking app I've used and it helps me do just that. In the beginning I was delighted by the light design of the app and insightful suggestions based on my answers. Now after 3 months it is starting to be a bit tiring to answer the same, sometimes vague questions 3 times a day. I mean the app asks if something has happened "ever" , "lately" or "in general" - 3 times a day! It also doesn't recognise stress-based problems, which I hope will be added in the future.

I've used this app for about 3 months now and I've come to the conclusion that it's not a good fit for me. The questions are vague and difficult to answer in a yes or no format, and they have no given time line to help base answers off of. The 15 day results is not an accurate portrayal of my moods.

This is the best app of its kind that I've tried so far out of three or four I've tried. It combines a mood tracker with occasional advice based on how you rate your mood throughout the day. The daily prompts encourage a bit of introspection and the advice is useful and often reassuring. I especially like that it doesn't try to shower me with positive thinking tropes; apps that do that don't help at all these days.
I just want to say that the insight are very helpful and inspiring. I like the fact that it ask you question 3 times a day. If you trying to track your mood and mental health I definitely recommend.
Leave me alone three times a day is too much. Perhaps twice a day morning and night okay

I really do like this app but have to give it only 4 stars. I think the questions are insightful but if you fail to answer all 3 sets of questions each day for 14 days then you do not get a rating of your mental health.
I think I am improving. Mindoc helps me see my feelings throughout the day. When something significant happens, I can observe how it is affecting my life. I've been doing mindoc for 3 months.
I've been using a couple of days and it helps to track how I've been feeling with simple questions asked 3 times daily. More confident than other apps I've used for the same purpose.

This will definitely be helpful if you are already in therapy or just starting therapy. It also works well to help you gauge where you are if you don't have access to therapy.

There's not enough space for me to tell whats all wrong with this. Definitely not fit for pregnant women. Whats considered normal during pregnancy is depression to them after 3 days of a 14 day assessment. It's the same vague questions everytime with no room for explanation. Very inaccurate. But supposedly if you want accurate you can pay for it! Not a mood tracker like I thought, its more of a "lets let an AI diagnose you" type thing.

A great way to journal your moods throughout the day. I think it's worth the quarterly sub if you're interested in their courses and lessons. Very insightful. It helps me to understand how things in my life affect my emotions. I didn't realize how much my emotional state was acting as a warning to the unhealthy factors pulling on me. It's definitely not going to cure any disease, but it certainly is a good tool. It works like a journal and checks on me 3 times a day.

He feels the same. It's made us stronger and has given us a better understanding of each other. Obviously it's not souly for couples but bottom line is that it's effective. Highly recommend.
I loved it. Until it started to no longer give me notification. And if i go in manualyy, there are no questions. The page comes up blank.
The timing is off, so it keeps asking questions in the middle of the night. No way to adjust it apparently? Useless if so.
This would be 5 stars if the question reminders didn't randomly stop popping up.
Decent app when I can actually use it, but it gives up way too easily on prompting me to answer its questions.

This app broke after I got my subscription. It misses notifications for logging, closes at random, and I frequently get stuck in question loops when I track. Aside from that, the app is solely focused on depression and anxiety and doesn't look at other reasons why someone might have trouble getting things done, concentrating, or any number of things. If you're disabled or neurodivergent, the app is just going to continuously tell you that you're depressed. I regret my purchase very much.

I found this app while looking for something to help me log my mood and emotional issues, and a lot of what came up were journal apps or just like bad - to - good scales that didn't offer much exploration or insight into how I was doing. I'm genuinely really impressed by the extent to which the app goes to prompt you and explore how you're feeling, and I think it's definitely helped me to be more aware of how my mood changes over time. I'm glad I found it, highly recommend. (my therapist agrees)

Love this app except for one thing... Whenever I try to create an account, it doesn't let me create/type a password hence making me unable to make an account. I had to factory reset my tablet so I wanted to save my journal entries, alas I couldn't without an account. Pretty sad about that, but this app is really good for reminding yourself you have feelings. Sometimes life gets too busy that you forget about your emotions. Helped me cope while I found the right therapist for me.
It stops :/// when it is going to ask first question , before creating an account ://
When I first downloaded this app it was good but now not so much. Before I was able to add different emotions and situations but not anymore since you now have to subscribe which is silly now.
Fabulous app. Takes all the guesswork out of it but asking questions daily. Highly recommend

This app has helped me express how I feel. I have been going through so much and this app has helped. I truly do recommand this if you are gping through hard times. This app also has a personal theripist you can talk to if you need help with anything.

You have to be consistent with answering the questions, and honest with yourself about how bad (or good!) things are. The insights are sometimes unhelpful, and sometimes right on the dot. I've also been trying Sanvello, but MindDoc gets more use from me because the 3x a day questions keep me coming back and being consistent. No ads, as far as I can tell.

So lets give it the full initial 14 day, but I'll most likely return with a fifth star. I honestly think this is, not simply the best theropy app, but one of the greatest apps that I could have possible downloaded. Thanks for the genuine app-roach. *dunca
I love it :) it actually takes you answers into account and makes sure you get the right mental help if needed 10/10 would recommend.

Where this app outshines most others is it doesn't expect me to just ~remember~ to log my mood, and provides questions that can be answered and modified before submitting. The questions and the insights can get a little repetitive - especially if you've been treating your own issues for a while and have read all the self-help books out there - and it seems very easy to hit "critical emotional health", but it is a nice routine and it makes you think about how you're actually feeling at the time.
The new questions that are being added lately are absolutely mind-blowing. This is therapy.

this app has been so helpful to me. i'm so much more aware of how i feel generally and also what can trigger me feeling bad. it helps me practise gratitude for the life i live simply by describing what happened to make me happy. only things i don't like about the app are the questions (q) 'do you currently have a job' and the sex-based qs (about activity/interest in sex). as a student, the first q is unneeded for me. i hate the sex-based qs. i wish there was an option to stop them being asked.
One of the best decisions of my life. I'm so glad I downloaded this app. ♡

It's a good app that works as intended There is also a premium version that you can get if you want some additional content/help My only complaint is that after a certain time there is no way to fill in missing questions; sometimes I miss a deadline to make an entry but there is no option to fill it in even if I know exactly how I was feeling at the missed time. It's not too big of a problem, it's just a little frustrating when I don't have an opportunity to fill it out until after the deadline.
Helps to organize my thoughts and really think on what and how I feel

Hello! This app was recommended by my doctor to monitor my progress. So far questions are easy to answer and evaluate what are my current emotions. Just a question, how do I sync it to Manila timezone? It seems like it's currently in another timezone that's why I'm missing blocks. It's only my 2nd time to answer but yhe 3rd block for today is already filled. Also I'm not getting push notif even tho I have already allowed it in my phone.
This app is useful and makes me feel less lonely :p
used to be good before it changed to mind doc but now i answered the questions for 12 days and it reset, now i have to answer them for 12 more days
Insightful questions. Good discovery points based on your answers too. Confirmed a lot of what I thought about how I'm feeling.

Bad. Some questions are very offensive. Even though I give feedback that the question isn't relevant to me or that I feel uncomfortable answering it the app does nothing to remove it and to continue with the moodwork for the day you must answer it. I'm quite disappointed and I thought that the update will fix these issues.
The questions make me more mindful of my emotions.

Great app to measure my mood. Great to see how differently my mood changes but I feel like on bad days it worsens my mood by admitting to myself that I am feeling bad. But on good days, it is a good thing to say I am feeling good. Depends on the mindset really. Only one thing I would suggest is to remove or alter the question about sex drive, I am sexually abstinent so that question is not valid for me.

Moodpath used to be really helpful in tracking depressive episodes & evaluating the severity of my symptoms. Since the app became MindDoc though, the results seem are much less accurate and are in fact quite discouraging – I felt great for weeks straight, but it attestates me moderate do bad emotional health as soon as I give one negative answer within a week. I don't understand the goal of this shift and am considering to remove the app because I think the new negative bias could even have counterproductive effects. Such a shame.
I think i might always be depressed but this app is really cool ps you'll make it hang in there
The insights are really nice. The question blocks seem to change depending on previous answers.

I would highly recomend, has many insights that were good to read/listen to. Only bad thing is somehow whether on my end or develpers' end? Had to start over which is maybe a good thing so can see if I get the same or similar results. More time in now and am very pleased with the app. I entered into a study of depression through the app. Wasn't really keen on the idea of my location, travel habits etc. being tracked but may help myself and others and is voluntary and can quit at any time.

I opened this app out of curiosity but after a week or so the pain and grief from the loss of my mom 7 years ago, finally began to unleash to the next phase. In a time of deep seclusion and disparity the one thing I could count on 3x a day is your questions asking if I am doing ok! 🙂

It's good in keeping track of the mind health but always miss the question. Should have a force-push notification which the notification windows will always pop up on the screen until the question answered

Easy to navigate and export results to share with gp and other supporting services. Also easy to see patterns of daily tasks that need to be changed. I only find it annoying that sometimes in one day asks the same questions e.g. Did you sleep badly last night
All of my answers from the day before are suddenly missing. First time this happens, but it's really weird and so I'm only giving 4 stars.
Please, add a response "I have a therapist" to suicidal thoughts section so the app wouldn't try to make me contact someone everytime) lemme think about death bruv
It's a good tracking app but for some reason if i answer questions at 11 it says i answered it at 5 so it's not recording my night time questions

Only lets you choose a couple moods for the day and write a note. Asks questions if you feel worthless. Of course we do. Everything else including and tracking or courses is $48 USD which will remind you how worthless you really are when the pandemic has killed your business and you're broke. Ripoff. Uninstalled.
The only problem I have is that I have to redo my results because I don't answer enough questions but I feel the necessary amount of answered questions should be more lenient because of the plague.

I've been using this for about 4 months now since before it changed to MindDoc and the app is working the same if not better than before for me. I like that there seems to be added questions now. Its such an easy way to document daily mood changes and changes over longer periods of time and has helped me in this stupid stupid year to really know when I'm going through a negative time and not.

although the questions are repetitive, it really does give me a good outlook on how consistent the way i feel is. it feels nice to have a sort of professional way of being told, "you should look out for this".
I'm trying mindfulness and this app is ideal for prompting me to reflect on my mood through the day. Good selection of questions which really make you focus on any issues.
Make me feel better
Excellent so far, I'm just working my way through the beginning at the moment, answering quick questions am and pm, then I'll get my review and update 😀

This app is a very good jurnal and helped me across this 1 year of use ,but one thing I don't understand... As all the negative questions (especially those that reflect bad thoughts) are coloured in pink and the positives are in teal, why at the LGBTQ question the answer "no" is pink? It should be both teal as neither is wrong ,it makes you think that is wrong to be straight 😂. Please make them equal coloured!

It's okay. It works just fine and has a visually pleasing UI. But my biggest problem is how little nuance is in the questions. Everything is either yes or no, then how concerning the issue is to you. Just saying yes or no to questions asking how often I do X or how I feel about Y isn't detailed enough to reflect my actual circumstances. To people considering whether to download, you can try it for a while and see if you like it. But it's just not enough for me.

Its very good and helpful but theres some triggering questions. I always mark them as irrelevant but they always come back. Questions about interest in sex (I'm asexual and find any talk of sex uncomfortable) and questions about alcoh*l which is a huge trigger and sometimes it causes flashbacks. I had to lower. I mark the a*cohol questions are irrelevant and say I get them too often and they keep coming up. It's very triggering and I just started to leave the app completely when they show up.
The app asked great questions, and gave help when needed. And always has the help line available in the app.
Its nice to be able to track how im feeling on a daily basis, can see what makes me feel certain ways.

I am only starting moodpath but I can already tell that I am going to stick with it. I only have a couple problems with the app. I don't have good wifi and even if there are no questions I would still like to track my emotions. Plus you cant get rid of insights or questions even if you complain. You have to look at them all day! One insight happened to be an excuse my family uses to blame me for my problems and it made me tear up thinking about it but it was stuck there all day in plain view...
It helps me to write down my thoughts but it asks the same questions alot.

It has helped a lot, and as a person with smiling depression this app is perfect for keeping me up to date about my emotions and what's wrong and what's not, I do recommend this app to everyone. Just one thing to the developers of this app, you should add "did you download this app yourself, or did someone else downloaded it for you" as an opening question, because that gives an idea to the app if the user really wants help or just hopeless And just so you know, no ads

Perfect. Just because it's paying me attention, asking me how I feel and giving me feedbacks about my attitude and habita made me feel much better. If you are feeling really bad and passing through hard times you could use this app, at least try it, in my personal opinion I felt much better. Id speak english if u see any error, sorry.
Easy and quick questions that help probe into how you are feeling and help you reflect on your emotions and life.
Just giving me insight on myself and ways to help me out 😃. Loved to use this app and more than that I think it's a first productive app that I find which is addictive . Loved it
Find this ap really useful but I do find it frustrating its doesn't always remind me to answer the questions and when I'm mid-depression its hard to remember to myself.
It seems very helpful, and it makes me think a bit more about my day and my actual actions within my day now I'm working all the thought process part of it thank you very much.

this app is one of the most simple and helpful way of keeping track of your feelings and makes you have a bit of knowledge on what is that your feeling, also gives you exercises to help you control your emotions and not be control by them
Good to take time to self-reflect. App doesn't bother you too much other than to remind you to take time to think about hi you're feeling
Brilliant. I can use this with my doctors to make treatment changes and see my progress.

I personally really recommend it if you're searching for a mood diary. The questions help reflecting on your mood, and they are quick and easy to answer and usually on time with the reminders! Sometimes I seem to miss a notification, when I don't have time to answer them due to my working schedule, but that's not very important to me, since I still get to answer regularly
Sometimes doesn't notify me to do the questions but otherwise brilliant app. Gives me this chance to just sit and think abt how I'm feeling before it all builds up.

Every therapist appointment asks how I've felt over the past weeks and I never had an answer before. I could never remember because depression makes you forget. They always said to journal but I never had the motivation to. Moodpath made tracking my feelings and moods so much easier. It takes no time at all and asks me questions in a format that's easy for me to understand. Because of Moodpath I finally know how bad my mental illness is and can get the help I need.

"how are you feeling?" Honestly pretty vexxed with the constant notifications overlapping eachother. I get we can turn off notifications now through the OS but that doesnt mean you can make the app just spam notifications. Especially when you dont use it for 3+ months.
During Covid this has been a life line to stop ruminations by making me aware of my thought patterns.
This app is very good, I recommend you install it if you want to know how you feel every week or something.
Decent app. I'll make it 5 star after I see results..
even if it's for a moment, this app distracts me from some stressful stuff that i'm going through. it also makes me realise some things i wasn't aware of. i like it:)
I love this app it makes me think about things j never thought were signs of depression and ig makes you pin point exactly what's bothering you so you can deal with it
I can have a better picture on how I feel during the day. I definitely recommend it!
Great way to keep records of your daily routine, struggles and good times.

I didn't expect much of this app and downloaded it just out of curiosity. The on boarding was amazing and not really annoying unlike other apps, the design language is so distinct and clear, the graphics are appealing and estheticly round. What I was so surprised about is how I actually started wanting to answer the questions, as they are not boringly repeated, but sometimes strangely meet my thoughts and worries I have throughout the day. The cherry on top is that my insurance pays the therapy
Although I haven't had my answers yet, since it I haven't gone through the full cycle, the question give me peace

I love this app! Its non-intrusive, simple, and really insightful. It asks you a few questions a few times per day, and uses that information to give really good assesments of your emotional state. I have been using it for a couple of weeks, at the beginning not much, but after seeing the type of insights it gives I am very motivated to answer all questions to see how it evaluates my mood. I definitely recommend it if you are interesting in tracking your mood and emotional state.
Questions repetitive👎🏾 Answers available not always applicable👎🏾 Great interpretation of answers🌻 Uplifting, relevant, & useful insights🌻 Cute graphics🌻
Very pretty and useful. I can't think of any improvements for now. Thanks for making it!! 🥰

It's pretty good stuff. It occurs more often when it's about 30min or less from the time for the next set.
One thing I'd change (add actually) is a way to track your pill taking as well

I honestly have nothing against this app. Its giving me related topics that are actually helpful, and when you answer deep questions in a negative way, they provide a help line. Im slowly improving, thanks to this app!
Allows me to reflect on my day and gives me further help where it thinks I might need it :)
NOTHING works without internet. A lot of questions are repetitive, even those need internet. Answer options are limited. Insights are not very insightful
So so helpful makes so much far easier hadn't even realized I had needed this until I got it

I am very happy with the app, it has helped me a lot in my low times. Please fix this if you can. Thanks to the whole team working behind all the support this app provides.😚🤗
Please add angry/ rage also to negative feelings. That's one of the ways anxiety and depression represent. Wonderful app.

Simple questions, only takes a few minutes at most to answer them. It's a great way to reflect on your day and how you really feel about life. I'm starting to take control again, not saying it's because of this app but I did notice a big difference in myself after just a week of using it 😁
Until now it is a really nice experience this app. I'm trying to keep updating how I feel, i guess for better results and I think the app is pretty usefull.
This is something I really need right now. So glad I came across this app

Using this app has been helpful. Sone of the questions don't make sense to me, but they are limited. Sometimes after it tells there's questions ready,I do them and I feel a little better

Excellent mood tracker, especially because of the variety and amount of questions throughout the day. I have noticed a couple of things that didn't add up on the letter you generate to the doctor but the fact that you can even do that is pretty cool.
This app makes me happy and helps.me talk more deeply about my feelings. 👌👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼
Could provide a more variety of questions and different categories

So far this app is going pretty well , first thing i do when i wake up is litterally focus on answering the questions (ofc with truth) , am really happy with this app right now , so yeah i think people should actually give it a chance!

Moodpath has been pretty alright. It's simple to use and it has a nice layout. Also, when I swapped phones, I had to start all over and I was really torn between keeping the app or getting rid of it. Some way of being able to save progress to a Google account or something would be lovely.

Hey! I love the idea of the app but I have an issue. Its a great way to keep track of howyouve been feeling and i haven't gotten a review yet but i think they are going to give me some kind of psychologists outlook on howI've been doing after 14 days of answering the questions and keeping tabs on how ive been feeling and what ive been doing. The app told me I am depressed. I already knew that. It is still useful to chart emotions and some of the tips are handy. If you happen to ever answer that you ever have suicidal thoughts it quickly asks you more questions and even offers to have the phone call a hotline if needed. I did not need that but thought that was a good thing. It then reminds you where in the app that number is in case you ever need it.
Amazing! Really makes me aware of what I need to do to improve my quality of life.

I personally think this app build your inner health. It really cares about ur personal health. There's a question about suicide-related and provides you a call (even tough I didnt know how the call is because I never call cause I dont need it rn). But this app really increase your slef-care!
It does actually help, also with the insights. I'm kinda happy that i found this app
it makes me happy all the time :))
I'm starting to realize my feelings and moos
This app has helped me feel more relived and so much better about myself.
The app is excellent, however for some reason I never get any reminders at night time so I almost always miss at least one set of questions a day.
Haven't had it for the full two weeks, but it's been great so far! I love the way you can answer questions, and I can say that the insights and courses really give me some hope.

Edit- ive changed devices and must restart ALL progress made thru app. Not pleased Im not even fully through my first few weeks and i love this app! It is NOT a journal/diary unless you choose to do so! There are SO many premade selections, covering any topics, not to mention youre also allowed to create your own if needed, in negative, neutral, or positive impact on both activities or emotions! Im loving it so far, VERY user friendly. Notifications arent annoying. Great app for mental health.

I wish the app saved progress when answering the questions rather than just timing out and saying they've been missed - you lose data if you haven't reached the save button at the end, yet you can go back and edit any saved entries as required. This app is great and I love answering to the questions! But there is this one problem occuring recently that I am unable to change the midday notification custom time. Please help.
Very good! Mood path made me feel comfortable with expressing my feelings, it has helped me so so much!! 🥺💞

It glitches from time to time and the red dot on results won't disappear for some reason. Other than that love the app, it makes me take a moment to think about how I'm feeling which is being very helpful with managing my emotions and understanding them. I think it helps you the person understand your feelings and if it continues to be like this I'll update the rating to 5 stars

This is such a great app. It makes me feel like I'm improving at times and really reflect on my behaviour. It's free options are plentiful and I love how it makes sure to remind me at the right times.
Does its job, and makes me realize how I'm actually feeling
I think everyone should definitely do this to self reflect and to know when to reach out if ever needed

The constant questions that we have to answer actually help you to reflect on your feelings. They help me when I can't pinpoint exactly what I feel at the moment. I don't think it's diagnosis is accurate but it can just help in keeping a track of your everyday feelings without much effort.

I enjoy it, often repeats the questions and that's ok because answers may change as our moods do. Would be good to have broader comparison over time, but you'd need to pay for that and I don't think it's cheap, I couldn't justify it even though I find it useful to reflect periodically on each day.
After let's move on with the first question the app Does not respond I don't know what to do?

I was asked by my therapist to keep a journal about my moods my feelings and what I did for the day your app allows me to pick multiple moods that I'm feeling it one time without having to pick each and every one and rate each filling I also like that I can share your results with my doctors keep up the great work I have nothing negative to say about this app

This app has helped me so much. I'm the kind of person who holds all my feelings to myself because I don't want to be a burden to anyone. One day my fiancee went through it and saw how down and depressed I had been for the past couple months. We talked about my feelings for hours and I broke down sobbing in front of them. That event helped me talk about my feelings to them and I've never felt better in my entire life, especially having just escaped a terrible and abusive situation months prior.
Its helped me see that I have more positive days than I thought.

The idea behind this app is sound, but the execution is horrid. There are a couple of free courses. They barely skim the surface and are in dire need of some proofreading. The interface is simple to the point of being hard to navigate. It also seems to work in different ways in response to the same action. The mood tracking questions are ambiguous at times, the notifications don't push from time to time and more.

moodpath helps me reflect on past emotions, that way I can look for a way to change that in the future. It also helps me with expressing my feelings, even without a psychitherapist. I'd reccommend this for others that don't really have an outlet for their feelings yet, or are too scared to do so=)
This app asks questions ultimately trailing towards the diagnosis....also the subject is able to realise himself through the excursion of such questions.
Self reflection is the best growth we can do!! I am liking the results the Moodpath app has givin me so far, I can actually see how I have been feeling and make changes.

I kept forgetting to use this so my progress kept resetting. I'm back in the swing now and it feels kinda good to just sort of journal my thoughts in a way. Just wished when I said I woke up at 7, it didn't send me notifications at 7. It's like "come on guys, I'm still waking up". But I changed the recorded time so I can actually experience my day before I answer questions. It really did help me sort of tell when I had a depression coming on. I'm hoping it can help me keep track of coming growth
Pretty helpful, makes me think about hoe im feeling and the situations Im dealing with.

Overall a really great and helpful app for tracking moods. I find it really useful as it keeps tract and makes me realise how I am feeling and then gives a summary as to whether your depression is mild, severe etc. Always recommends to see a doctor!
Your app doesn't give notification or reminder with questions.

This app has been very helpful for me to track when I'm having negative thoughts and feelings. The app is really good and is helping me a lot. But I don't like the last update where you put your feelings as a tag and then specified if they are positive negative or neutral.
This app let's me focus on my feelings, and sometimes I have a rough time figuring out how I feel.

Pretty good so far, the daily questions are pretty helpful and keep you aware of the days progress and events and I find it very grounding. good, but get tired of the same question all the time.

It is helpful for me and I think it would do a lot of good to others. It helps me keep a clear mind and the questions are suitable and helps me reflect on myself and how I feel I'm actually doing. It gives something to vent to in a way with being able to put down your emotions and actually think about how you're feeling It's good 👍👍
I was suprised how I was realy feeling...

Really insightful questions and feedback. Also, real life help is available through this app. No ads.

It asks about your answer to some of the questions, they pretty much ask how much is it a burden to you instead of how often. I know I'm not the only one who's used to some of these feeling that happen

It can definitely help in turning things around but you have to acknowledge that it's an investment . I'be only used it briefly, but just being able to recognize patterns in thoughts and proximate causes makes a difference. The exercises are also helpful and a good resource for me when I need some kind of help.

I have been using this app for 3 weeks, after a bipolar 2 diagnosis when I hit the bottom w/anxiety. The questions aren't exactly the same each time it checks in with me & instead of asking a dozen questions (like the ones on the pre-visit questionnaire for my Drs.) it's just 3 questions each time. After the questions I can leave a comment to kind of explain my thoughts. And the positive/negative word choices to just kind of put with my mood. And the biweekly analysis are helpful. LOVE IT ALL
Sometimes I just wanna end it all but then I think about everyone. One day it'll win
I just...I don't want to make a whole monolog about this. But this app, really filled my time. And in a good way. It stopped me from killing myself.

This app is very insightful and professional. It helps me better understand certain feelings and how to help with negative feelings. All you have to do is answer some questions and tell how you're feeling throughout the day.

Helps too answer questions daily & the assessments and what to do are interesting and useful

So far so good. I've tried out oodles of these sorts of apps to try and find tools to help long-standing depression, and this is the best I've tried so far. Of course, there is no quick solution for these problems, but this app has enough detail to matter, and doesn't come across as patronising in the process. I like the auto diary feature, and the articles are interesting and informative.
Sehr gute App. Empfehlenswert
So far so good . New to this application and love it. I like the daily prompts and checklists. It will be interesting to read the results when the 14 days are up. Great!
Neat app, asks interesting questions, A great tool for introspection.
Es ist wirklich hilfreich einfach mal zu sagen was man denkt wenn man nicht mit anderen darüber reden kann.🙂🙏💍

It has only been a few days but it feels nice to check in with myself in the morning, afternoon and evening. It's less demanding than having a journal. It allows me to be honest with myself. It is interesting to see how much my mood changes. So far I am enjoying it.
It's been interesting seeing my patterns over time. I'm planning on continuing to use it because I want to see if I can learn to better recognize my patterns on my own.
